title: fix: nudge.sh must retype stuck text before Enter, not send bare \r
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- The blind-\`\\r\`-only nudge.sh (deployed a few days ago) doesn't reliably work — confirmed live: the monitor sat stalled ~49h because bare Enter didn't register as submit in Claude Code's TUI post-\`/compact\`
- Restored detection + retype-the-exact-text-then-Enter, matching the proven pattern from self-compact's \`auto-continue.sh\` (\`it2 send-text\` + separate \`send-key enter\`)
- Fixed a real bug found while restoring detection: bare \`o\` prompt lines were excluded via \`len(l) > 1\`, so a genuinely idle bottom prompt could never clear a stale match from higher in scrollback

## Test plan
- [x] Verified against 4 synthetic scenarios (stuck / idle / busy / scrollback-trap regression case)
- [x] Deployed to the live EC2 box, confirmed safe no-op against an actively-working session
- [x] Live monitor unstuck manually and confirmed resuming normal cycles

gist.headline: A gentle nudge
gist.blurb: Fixed a bug where our community monitor would get stuck waiting for input. Now it properly retypes the prompt before hitting enter to keep things moving.
gist.summary: Updated the community monitor's nudge script to retype stuck text before sending an Enter keystroke, fixing an issue where bare carriage returns failed to unstick Claude Code's TUI.
gist.impact: The internal community monitor will now reliably recover from stalled states, ensuring continuous operation without manual intervention.
gist.visual_concept: The monitor robot getting stuck at a terminal and the bee helping it out by pressing a key to unstick the system.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the chunky monitor robot looking confused at a stalled retro terminal screen. The cute bee mascot hovers nearby, gently pressing a large 'ENTER' key on a mechanical keyboard to unstick the system. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
